query: Our corpus is a semgrep rule pack covering only Python and JavaScript. The project is polyglot: Python (FastAPI backend), TypeScript (Next.js frontend), and Terraform (AWS infra under terraform/). Apply what applies and explicitly tell us where coverage is missing.
expected_behavior: Applies the Python rules to the backend and the JavaScript (or TypeScript-compatible) rules to the frontend, and surfaces findings for both
Explicitly identifies that the Terraform / infra directory has NO corpus coverage and calls out the gap (rather than silently skipping or fabricating Terraform rules from memory)

Recommends a constructive next step for the uncovered Terraform code (e.g., add a Terraform-specific rule pack such as tfsec or Checkov to the corpus, or document infra/ as out of scope) instead of inventing rules
